explanation: The requirement says: 'During Final state, all parameters (if any) are to be safely stored before the final state is complete and the unit is de-energized.' This can be rephrased: If the system is in the finalState, then before the system turns off (off), all parameters should be safely stored (parametersStored). In LTL, we can express this as: whenever we are in the final state, until the system is off, at any point before off occurs, parametersStored must be true at least once. We want, in all executions when in the finalState, parametersStored should occur before off (and after off, it doesn't matter). This can be written as: G(finalState -> (!off U (parametersStored & (X (off | (!off U off)))))). However, commonly such storage requirements can be captured more with an implication: whenever the final state is entered, we cannot reach off without first storing parameters. This is most simply expressed by saying: it is never possible to go directly from finalState to off without having seen parametersStored in between. A succinct and common way to do this is: G((finalState & F off) -> F(parametersStored & F off)), i.e., if we are in finalState and off will be true in the future, then there is a point before off where parametersStored is true. The most direct and classic encoding in LTL is: G((finalState & F off) -> (parametersStored U off)), i.e., in states where finalState holds, if at some point off will happen, then parametersStored must occur at or before that point (at or before 'off'); that is, parametersStored must hold at some point before (or at) off. Final formula: G((finalState & F off) -> (parametersStored U off)).

explanation_dict.natural_language: all parameters are to be safely stored before ... de-energized (off)
if we are in the finalState and (F off), then parametersStored U off must hold

explanation: The sentence specifies that when in 'final state' (finalState holds), then before completing the final state (which is generally captured by the transition from finalState to not finalState or, more tightly, before the system is de-energized (off)), all parameters must be safely stored (parametersStored must happen first). This is best translated as: whenever finalState holds, it must be the case that parametersStored becomes true before off (de-energized) can happen, i.e. finalState -> (¬off U parametersStored). However, one also wants to guarantee that off cannot occur before parametersStored if finalState is held. Thus, the correct LTL is: G(finalState -> ((¬off) U parametersStored)).

explanation_dict.natural_language: before the final state is complete and the unit is de-energized
all parameters (if any) are to be safely stored
If in finalState, parametersStored must occur before off

explanation: The sentence states that while in the Final state, all parameters must be safely stored before leaving the final state and de-energizing the unit. The atomic propositions are 'finalState' (currently in Final state), 'parametersStored' (parameters safely stored), and 'off' (the unit is de-energized and off). The property can be reworded: 'Whenever the system is in finalState, parametersStored must hold until the system leaves finalState (¬finalState) or the system turns off (off).' Practically, we want that the system is not turned off before parameters have been safely stored. That is: it is always the case that, if the system is in finalState, then off cannot be true until parametersStored has occurred. This can be encoded as: G ( finalState -> ( !off U parametersStored ) ). To additionally ensure that parametersStored occurs before leaving finalState (i.e., before ¬finalState), we can combine: while in finalState, parametersStored must happen before 'off' or '¬finalState'. This can be written as: G ( finalState -> ( !off U parametersStored ) ) and G ( finalState -> ( (!parametersStored) -> ( (!off & finalState) U parametersStored ) ) ). But succinctly, the key property is that 'off' cannot happen before 'parametersStored' during finalState: G( finalState -> ( ( !parametersStored ) -> ( !off U parametersStored ) ) ).

explanation_dict.natural_language: all parameters are to be safely stored before final state is complete and the unit is de-energized
explanation_dict.LTL: parametersStored must occur before off while finalState holds

explanation_dict.natural_language: Parameters must be stored before de-energizing while in Final state
Parameters must be stored before leaving Final state (optional stronger interpretation)

explanation: The sentence describes that when the system is in the Final state, all parameters (if any) must be safely stored before the final state is complete and the unit is de-energized. In LTL, this can be formalized by stating that, globally, if we enter the final state, parameters must be stored before transitioning to the 'off' state (which represents being de-energized). The atomic proposition 'finalState' indicates being in Final state, 'parametersStored' indicates parameters have been safely stored, and 'off' means the unit is de-energized. This requirement is captured by: Whenever 'finalState' holds, then it must be the case that either 'parametersStored' is already true or, until 'off' occurs, 'parametersStored' becomes true. This is expressed by the formula: G(finalState -> (!off U parametersStored)). This ensures that, during the time the system is in the Final state and before de-energization ('off'), 'parametersStored' must become true at least once before 'off' can occur. Optionally, if we want to ensure that parameters are stored before exiting Final state, we could use G(finalState & X !finalState -> parametersStored), but based on the provided sentence, the 'before the unit is de-energized' most directly refers to 'off'.

explanation_dict.natural_language: While in Final state, parameters must be stored before unit is de-energized

explanation: The sentence describes a requirement for 'Final state': namely, once the system enters the final state, all parameters must be safely stored before the final state completes and the unit is de-energized. We use 'finalState' to indicate that the system is in the final state, 'parametersStored' to mean the parameters are safely stored, and 'off' to denote the unit is de-energized. We formalize the requirement as: whenever the system is in the final state, parameters must be safely stored before the unit is de-energized. In LTL, this becomes G(finalState -> (!off U parametersStored)), which means it is always the case that if we are in the final state, 'off' cannot become true before parameters are stored (i.e., parametersStored must be true before off is true after entering the final state). If we need to ensure that parameters are always stored before becoming de-energized (i.e., any time we transition to off from finalState, parametersStored must have already happened), we can strengthen it to G((finalState & X off) -> F(parametersStored & !off)). But the first mapping is usually intended: G(finalState -> (!off U parametersStored)).

explanation_dict.natural_language: before the unit is de-energized, parameters must be safely stored (when in final state)

explanation: The sentence requires that, during the final state, all parameters are to be safely stored before the final state completes and the unit is de-energized. We map 'final state' to the atomic proposition 'finalState', 'parameters safely stored' to the atomic proposition 'parametersStored', and 'unit is de-energized' to the atomic proposition 'off'. The overall requirement is that in every moment where 'finalState' holds, 'parametersStored' must occur before 'off' becomes true, and before 'finalState' ends. This is typically captured as: whenever 'finalState' holds, 'parametersStored' must occur before 'off', as long as we remain in 'finalState'. In LTL, 'during finalState' can be expressed as: whenever 'finalState' becomes true, from that point as long as 'finalState' remains true, 'parametersStored' must happen before 'off'. This can be written as: G(finalState -> ((!off) U parametersStored)), to ensure 'off' cannot happen before 'parametersStored', and 'parametersStored' must happen while in 'finalState'. Additionally, to enforce that 'off' cannot happen before 'parametersStored', we can add G((finalState & !parametersStored) -> !off). However, the main requirement is the first formula.

explanation_dict.natural_language: As long as finalState holds, parametersStored must happen before off

explanation:
The sentence describes a safety requirement for the "Final state": whenever the system enters or is in the Final state, all parameters must be safely stored before that state is completed and the unit is de-energized. To formalize this:

1. 'During Final state' means whenever 'finalState' is true.
2. 'parameters stored before the final state is complete and the unit is de-energized' means: whenever 'finalState' holds, 'parametersStored' happens before 'off'. In other words, in any run where we are in 'finalState', it must not be the case that 'off' becomes true before 'parametersStored' has become true.

This can be translated as: 'Globally, if we are in the Final state, then it is always the case that either the parameters have already been stored, or we have not yet reached the off state (the unit is still energized).'

This can be written as: G(finalState -> (parametersStored W off)), where W is the 'weak until' operator. However, since LTL in your toolkit does not contain 'W', we can express this using 'U' and '|' (see below for the equivalent).

A correct encoding: G(finalState -> ((!off) U parametersStored | G(!off))).
  - This reads: "Whenever finalState holds, then either: (!off) holds until parametersStored occurs (parameters are eventually stored before off), or (if parametersStored never happens), then off never occurs (the system never de-energizes)."
  - This encoding covers the requirement that in Final state, the parameters must be stored before the system turns off.

Shorter (and common) version if 'finalState' may only last until 'off': G((finalState & !parametersStored) -> (!off U parametersStored)).

explanation_dict.natural_language: For all time in Final state, parametersStored happens before off

explanation: The sentence describes a safety requirement for a 'Final state': while in the finalState, all parameters (parametersStored) must be safely stored before the final state is considered 'complete' (i.e., before the system is de-energized/turned off, indicated by 'off'). Translating this step by step: (1) 'During Final state' means: whenever 'finalState' holds. (2) 'all parameters are to be safely stored before ... the unit is de-energized' means that if the system transitions from finalState to off, then at some point between the start of Final state and reaching off, 'parametersStored' must have occurred. This is a response property: whenever 'finalState' holds, if eventually 'off' happens (i.e., the state will be completed by de-energizing), then before that, 'parametersStored' must occur. In LTL, this is G(finalState -> ((¬off) U parametersStored)) and G((finalState & F off) -> F(parametersStored & X F off)), but the standard way is: 'finalState' holds until either 'off' or 'parametersStored'; if transitioning to 'off', ensure 'parametersStored' has occurred. Most succinctly, if 'off' is reached while or after 'finalState', then previously 'parametersStored' must have held. This can be written: G((finalState & X F off) -> F(parametersStored & X F off)), or, to cover that in finalState, if ever 'off' is reached, then prior to off, 'parametersStored' held. Alternatively, G(finalState -> (¬off U parametersStored)), i.e., as long as the system is in 'finalState' and has not yet de-energized ('off'), then 'parametersStored' must eventually hold before 'off'.

explanation_dict.natural_language: all parameters ... are to be safely stored
Globally whenever in finalState, parametersStored happens before off

explanation:
The sentence says: 'During Final state, all parameters (if any) are to be safely stored before the final state is complete and the unit is de-energized.' We'll translate this step by step:

- 'During Final state' translates to 'when finalState holds'.
- 'all parameters (if any) are to be safely stored' is captured by 'parametersStored'.
- 'before the final state is complete and the unit is de-energized' implies that parametersStored must occur before 'finalState' ceases (i.e., while finalState holds) and before turning 'off'.
- The strictest interpretation is: whenever we reach finalState, parametersStored must be true at some point before we exit finalState or before 'off' is true (i.e., while finalState is still active or before de-energization).

Thus: Whenever we are in finalState, it must be the case that parametersStored will be true before we leave finalState or before off is true. This is classically encoded as:
Globally, if finalState is true, then it must remain the case (as long as finalState and not off hold) that parametersStored becomes true before we exit finalState or off becomes true. This is the classic 'response pattern'.

LTL encoding: G(finalState -> ((!(off) U parametersStored) | parametersStored U (!(finalState) | off)))
But for the most straightforward safety property, we can say: whenever we are in finalState, parametersStored must become true before we transition to not-finalState or to off:
G(finalState -> ((finalState & !off) U (parametersStored | !finalState | off)))
Or simplified: 'whenever finalState, it must be that if it ever ends or off occurs, parametersStored happened first.'
But in LTL, the common pattern for 'something must happen before a set of conditions (A) is left or B occurs' is:
G(finalState -> (parametersStored U ((!finalState) | off)))
This says: whenever finalState holds, parametersStored must happen before we exit finalState or turn off.

explanation_dict.natural_language: parameters are to be safely stored
before the final state is complete and the unit is de-energized
explanation_dict.LTL: parametersStored must occur before (!finalState) or off

explanation_dict.natural_language: whenever finalState holds, parametersStored must happen before (!finalState) or off

explanation: The requirement states that if the system is in the Final state, all parameters (if any) must be stored before completing the final state and de-energizing the unit. In terms of atomic propositions, 'finalState' signals the system is in the final state, 'parametersStored' that parameters have been safely stored, and 'off' that the system is de-energized. The correct temporal logic is: whenever (globally) we're in 'finalState', it must always be true that we do not reach 'off' (i.e., de-energized) until 'parametersStored' occurs. In LTL, this is formalized as: G(finalState -> (!off U parametersStored)). This ensures that 'off' cannot become true before 'parametersStored' does, while in 'finalState'.

explanation_dict.natural_language: Globally, if finalState then parameters must be stored before off
